name: mc_insufficient_failsafe_insufficient_history_below_stop
description: AC-2 fail-safe proof (audit HIGH). A 15-day history is below MC_MIN_HISTORY_DAYS (20), so run_monte_carlo cannot compute a real Monte Carlo probability. The position is also deeply below its protective trailing stop. The two facts together are the dangerous case: the protective stop MUST still fire even though the MC second opinion is uncomputable. Pre-fix, run_monte_carlo emits the in-band value 100.0 ('definitely beats benchmark'), compute_exit_confirmation's MC sanity gate (prob_beating < 60.0) is therefore False, below_stop_condition is permanently False, and the trailing-stop exit can NEVER confirm while MC history is insufficient -- insufficient data DISABLES the protective stop (fail-dangerous). Post-fix, run_monte_carlo signals 'insufficient' via a distinct out-of-band sentinel (None) and compute_exit_confirmation treats it as 'MC confirmation unavailable' -- the stop fires on the ticks-below-stop condition alone.
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history (15 days, below the 20-day MC minimum). The exit-confirmation inputs are named scalars, not producer-computed floats.

exit_confirmation_scenario.description: Position deeply below the protective stop, armed, not triggered. Driven for EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS consecutive ticks while MC history is insufficient. The protective exit must confirm.

contract.run_monte_carlo: Returns a DISTINCT out-of-band insufficient-history sentinel (None) -- NOT the in-band 100.0. The test asserts the return is None / not a probability in [0,100].
contract.compute_exit_confirmation: When prob_beating is the insufficient sentinel, the MC sanity gate is treated as 'unavailable' and does NOT veto the exit. With current_return deeply below stop_trigger_level - MAGNITUDE_FLOOR_PCT for EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS consecutive ticks, is_trailing_stop_hit becomes True.

derivation: MAGNITUDE_FLOOR_PCT and EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS are module constants read from math_engine at test time -- not hardcoded. The magnitude condition (current_return -5.0 <= stop_trigger_level -1.0 minus MAGNITUDE_FLOOR_PCT) is satisfied; with the MC gate not vetoing, the count increments each tick and the stop fires on the EXIT_CONFIRM_TICKS-th tick. Insufficient MC data must fail toward PROTECTING the position.
